3.name: Construct Companion
3.html: Once per short rest, spend 3 Forge Charges to create a combat construct from your lattice material. The construct has HP equal to your Blood Smith level × 2, defense, and on its turn can make one attack dealing 5 + your FRAME bludgeoning damage. It lasts 1 minute or until destroyed. You take 5 resonant damage when the construct is created. The construct acts on your initiative.

6.name: Rapid Structure
6.html: You can create External Forge structures as a bonus action. Additionally, your structures gain HP equal to your class expertise bonus × 3 (in addition to the standard amount). You can spend 1 Forge Charge as a free action to repair one of your active structures for 5 HP.

10.name: Complex Architecture
10.html: Your External Forge structures can now include mechanical components: a structure can have a hinged section (a door), a trap component (a pressure-trigger that deals 7 damage and triggers on non-you creatures that step on it), or a signal relay (a Wire Craft amplification point that extends your communication range by 60 feet while active). Each additional component costs 1 extra Forge Charge and 1 additional round of work.

14.name: The Battlefield Rewritten
14.html:
Once per long rest, spend 8 Forge Charges to perform a Battlefield Sculpt — a full-scale reshaping of a 30-foot radius area that takes 1 full minute of work. You can: create difficult terrain from solidified lattice-metal, construct a full defensive position (four wall segments, a roof, and internal supports), create a network of trap-triggered structures, or raise a structure large enough to serve as tactical high ground. You take 14 resonant damage from the creation. The result lasts for 1 hour. Within this sculpted area, allied creatures gain +3 to EDGE resistance checks (they know the terrain; enemies don't). The structures built this way have your class expertise bonus × 5 HP each.

---

system.description: The Sculptor has taken Blood Smithing outward — beyond their body, beyond weapons and armor, into the creation of external structures from their blood and lattice-metal. They are battlefield artificers who build under fire: walls, bridges, distractions, constructs that serve a function and are discarded when the function is done. The cost is visible in their work — every structure is stained with the blood of its creation, and the Sculptor's enhancement scans show more lattice gaps than any other Blood Smith path, because they are constantly exporting pieces of themselves into the environment and waiting for the body to rebuild.
